Money transfer

Send money instantly 24/7, locally or even abroad. Support your family, help out a friend or pay back in a few taps. Fin.do money transfer system is not an e-wallet, there is no need to top up your account or to cash out, money goes directly from sender to recipient.

Seems quite cool, I've already integrated into our Slack. Looking forward to seeing how it adapts to our team and sharing the feedback. Good luck! :) @ajain

Ad Intel

Ad Intel

@stefani\_kovachevska Thanks! Please provide us any and all feedback. We'd be very grateful.

Report

Heather Lozano [...] Report

 2 views

•1 review

I use recess every day with one of my teams. It helps keep casual conversation going, and reduces the sense of friction if you wanted to reach out to one of them for any work related question. While I do not know that they're having genuine bonding experiences, I have definitely learned some more pieces of information about my colleagues, and I've gained a better sense of them as people instead of just peers. And yes, there has definitely been some laughter and play with it as well. :-) I have also tried using recess with a different team and had no success whatsoever, as no one else was interested in the 10 seconds it took to answer the simple questions. Even with using the reminder feature, it simply did not take. So, YMMV.

#### What's great [...] Subscribe

4.9

Based on 19 reviews

Review Recess?

Reviewers say Recess works best as a lightweight way for remote and distributed teams to stay connected through prompts, icebreakers, and small games. People repeatedly praise its simple setup, low-pressure async format, flexible timing, and mix of playful and more thoughtful questions, saying it helps teammates learn about each other, laugh a bit, and makes work conversations feel easier. A small note of caution: engagement depends on the team. For some groups it becomes a lasting habit, while others lose interest or use it less once the team is already established.

Brad Hipps [...] Report

Brad Hipps

Socratic

Socratic

Maker

@jamie\_dowis Understood! We actually tried in a previous life to do what you suggest--essentially, to "overlay" existing task tools--and the results weren't great. It's tough to provide users actionable intelligence, at the time they most need it, when you aren't the system where the work is actually being done. That said, we do integrate with git (GitHub, GitLab), not only to automate task movement, but to enrich our own work intelligence with analytics we build from code activity. We will definitely do the same with other complementary systems in software dev-- CI/CD comes to mind.

Report

Roop Reddy

Congratulations on the launch! Every time I use Jira, I always think why there is no better tool than Jira. This looks like a solution [...] Socratic started from a single, lasting frustration. Every task tool we've ever used was high tax, low return. In those tools, you do all the data entry, and then you do all the sorting, sifting, and spelunking to try to understand the state of work. We wondered: what if we flipped the equation? What if we made a task system that works for you? What if we built something that wasn't just Jira with nicer lipstick, something that was much more than (as one early Socratic user described Jira), "A form UI over a database that tells me nothing about the way we work"? Enter Socratic. Socratic harnesses your work activity and shows ways of working easier, faster, better—all with no manual inputs. Your daily activity drives Socratic's insights; real-time insights drive stronger work health. Think

I use it for my team. Each week we use the Recess icebreaker question in our team meeting to help connect. Its nice that as the team lead I don't have to invent something and can trust that what Recess serves up with be good. For offsites we use the game room so we can play mini games together. We typically are on a VC at the same time so we can talk trash while playing ;)

results.title: Best Payroll / PEO / EOR / HRIS for Distributed Teams? : Deel Discussion Forums | Product Hunt
results.content:
I could see the % of their last raise and when it was and I didn’t even have to calculate it myself!). Beyond that, I was in love with how easy it was for me create custom fields, custom reports and dashboards - and the best part was… I could EDIT the data INSIDE the view. No going to dig up the field on the person’s profile I needed to make edits. I hate to call it that, but basically it was a excel/google sheets on steroids because it synced to my employee database. Isn’t this how it SHOULD be?! [...] If either of you find it helpful, I can connect you directly with the Deel CEO & co-founder, Alex as he's always happy to help! You can email me at operations @ producthunt.com and I'm happy to connect you.

Gabe Perez
Product Hunt

Product Hunt

Really rad thorough breakdown.   
  
I've used @Gusto, @Remote, and @Deel and I personally really enjoy Deel. It's a really comprehensive experience that works really well. Not to mention it costs $0 to transfer from Deel to @Wise which makes it realllly easy to be a working nomad.

I agree with you on @Lattice - it's pretty powerful but I do think it could help managers and leadership by being a bit more proactive vs needing someone always input/ask for employee input. [...] Julie Modad

@claire\_baker2 I completely agree with your review. As someone relatively new to Deel (a few months in), I initially started using them for domestic payroll with plans to expand internationally. Unfortunately, my experience has mirrored your feedback (last paragraph).

The onboarding process was clunky, with confusing and non-intuitive solutions. However, the biggest disappointment has been customer support. They send frequent but unhelpful "updates," and after days of waiting for a real answer, they often just redirect the issue back to the CSM instead of providing a concrete solution. For anything beyond a basic, out-of-the-box issue, getting real help feels like a massive uphill battle.

Overall, I’m not impressed and wouldn’t consider myself a fan.
